mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive forward returns for the S&P 500 over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window or forward returns are negative in 60% of rate-cut months.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.4 percentage points signals sudden labor market deterioration, triggering immediate risk-off selling and negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.4pp in a single month
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE rises above the 0.4pp threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand growth, driving corporate earnings expectations and positive quarterly equity returns.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both exceed their 12-month moving averages
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60-day returns with a statistical significance level of p < 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during a late-cycle expansion phase reflects capacity utilization nearing limits, prompting inflation fears that compress equity multiples and generate negative forward returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration in late-cycle regimes yields positive 20-day returns in over 65% of observations, contradicting the inflation-fear compression mechanism.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real GDP growth rates directly correlate with improved corporate profit margins, producing consistently positive short-term S&P 500 returns as markets price in stronger earnings.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDPC1 growth shows a negative or statistically insignificant correlation with 5-day forward returns over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Core inflation exceeding 3.0% forces the Federal Reserve to accelerate rate hikes, increasing borrowing costs and discount rates that suppress equity valuations over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 60-day returns in more than 60% of periods where CPIAUCSL growth surpasses the 3.0% threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a falling unemployment rate confirms robust labor market tightening, boosting consumer spending forecasts and generating positive monthly equity returns.
failure_condition: The joint PAYEMS-UNRATE signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark with a Sharpe ratio above 0.5 over a 3-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp contraction in industrial production during a post-shock recovery phase signals persistent supply chain bottlenecks, triggering immediate market pessimism and negative weekly returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO contractions in post-shock recovery periods are followed by positive 5-day returns in over 70% of cases, invalidating the bottleneck pessimism channel.
